model.reasoning_summary: HIP-G submission #5. WEIGHTED prediction ensemble. weights [1,1,1,3] -> #4 (residual spectrogram, the most AP-balanced spec over 20 seeds, AP=124/144) gets 0.5 of the vote, #1/#2/#3 each get ~0.167. The earlier [3,1,1,1] was based on a single-seed lottery roll that made #1 look balanced (148/140/144 once); the 20-seed expected behavior of spec #1 is actually AP-starved (52/144). #4 is the principled AP-protector. Weights are NOT val-tuned (12 subjects -> overfit); chosen from the spec-level expected per-class behavior measured at n=20 seeds.

name: submission_05_prediction_ensemble
reasoning_summary: HIP-G submission #5. WEIGHTED prediction ensemble. weights [3,1,1,1] -> #1 (the class-balanced spectrogram champion) gets 0.5 of the vote, #2/#3/#4 each get ~0.167. The uniform 4-way average (run earlier) was AP-starved: #2/#3/#4 all collapse Arm Pain and 3-to-1 outvoted #1. Weighting #1 up protects AP while still letting #2-#4 (which are stronger on NP/HP) contribute. weights are normalized internally.
